competency_groups.title: Conceptual Foundations and Measurement
competency_groups.description: Equip learners with tools to understand the working of Chemistry, develop conceptual foundations, represent phenomena at macroscopic/molecular/symbolic levels, identify patterns, and apply knowledge to real-life situations.
competencies: Understand nature of matter, properties, measurement, uncertainty, laws of chemical combination, Dalton's atomic theory
Calculate atomic/molecular masses, mole concept, molar mass, percentage composition, stoichiometry

competency_groups.title: Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to trace the development from sub-atomic particles discovery through Bohr's model to the quantum mechanical model.
competencies: Explain discovery of sub-atomic particles, atomic models, developments leading to Bohr's model

competency_groups.title: Periodic Classification and Trends
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify elements, understand periodic law, electronic configurations, block types, and trends in properties.
competencies: Explain genesis of periodic classification, modern periodic law, nomenclature for Z>100
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding Theories
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to apply various bonding theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital) and understand hybridization.
competencies: Apply Kossel-Lewis approach, Ionic/Electrovalent bond concepts

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics and Spontaneity
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to analyze thermodynamic terms, enthalpy changes, calorimetry, spontaneity, Gibbs energy change, and equilibrium.
competencies: Analyze applications of thermodynamics, measurement of ∆U and ∆H

competency_groups.title: Equilibrium Principles
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand dynamic equilibrium, constants (K), reaction quotient (Q), Gibbs energy relationship, and factors affecting equilibria.
competencies: Analyze homogeneous/heterogeneous equilibria, ionic equilibrium in solution

competency_groups.title: Redox and Electrode Processes
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify oxidation/reduction in terms of electron transfer, assign oxidation numbers, and relate redox to electrode processes.
competencies: Classify classical ideas vs. electron transfer concepts

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Fundamentals and Mechanisms
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent organic compounds, classify them, apply IUPAC nomenclature, understand isomerism, reaction mechanisms, and purification techniques.
competencies: Apply structural representations, classification, nomenclature
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbon Classification and Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify hydrocarbons, understand properties of alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, aromatic compounds, and assess carcinogenic/toxicity.
competencies: Classify and analyze properties of Alkanes, Alkenes, Alkynes

competency_groups.title: Solution Chemistry and Colligative Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to express concentration, understand solubility, vapor pressure, ideal/non-ideal solutions, and determine molar mass via colligative properties.
competencies: Apply concepts of types of solutions, expression of concentration

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry and Energy Conversion
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand electrochemical cells, galvanic cells, Nernst equation, conductance, electrolytic cells, batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion.
competencies: Analyze operation of Galvanic Cells and Electrolytic Cells

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics and Reaction Rates
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to analyze rate of reaction, factors influencing it, integrated rate equations, temperature dependence, and collision theory.
competencies: Apply concepts of rate determination and temperature effects

competency_groups.title: d-and f-Block Elements Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand position in periodic table, electronic configuration of d-block elements, general properties, important compounds, lanthanoids, actinoids.
competencies: Analyze general properties and applications of Transition Elements

competency_groups.title: Coordination Compounds Theory
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to apply Werner's theory, understand nomenclature, isomerism, bonding (including metal carbonyls), and applications.
competencies: Apply definitions of terms pertaining to Coordination Compounds

competency_groups.title: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify, name, understand C-X bond nature, preparation methods (haloalkanes/haloarenes), physical properties, chemical reactions.
competencies: Analyze classification and nomenclature of halo compounds

competency_groups.title: Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify compounds, apply nomenclature rules, understand structures of functional groups, and properties.
competencies: Differentiate between Alcohols, Phenols, and Ethers

competency_groups.title: Carbonyl Compounds Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand nomenclature/structure of carbonyl/carboxyl groups, preparation methods, physical properties, chemical reactions.
competencies: Analyze Preparation and Uses of Aldehydes/Ketones

competency_groups.title: Amines Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand structure, classification, nomenclature, preparation methods (including Diazonium salts), physical properties.
competencies: Analyze Chemical Reactions and Importance of Diazonium Salts

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify and understand the structure and function of carbohydrates, proteins, enzymes, vitamins, nucleic acids, hormones.
competencies: Identify components of Biomolecules

competency_groups.title: Problem Solving and Scientific Inquiry
competency_groups.description: Inculcate problem-solving skills, integrate life skills/values in context of chemistry, contribute to frontier research areas (climate change, environment), interface with other disciplines.
competencies: Apply acquired knowledge to solve problems in new situations
